<mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-53" value="ms appears to allow extra roles to be assigned - see doc on gdrive" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-55" value="During initial sign-in in an abstratium app, the user is redirected to abstrauth. Abstrauth knows the clientId and hence the application. After creating the org, it can check if the client allows automatic subscription and if it does, it can create that subscription.&nbsp;<div>The JWT contains the orgId and that can be used as the tenantId in Hibernate.</div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="30" y="948" width="250" height="150" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-56" value="Paid features can be based on the user having specific roles. The roles are assigned during sign-in, by using the subscription to check the contract and its billing status. If the user has credit, has prepaid or we trust them to post-pay, then the extra roles are added to the JWT and the application can trust that they may use those features." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-260" y="888" width="250" height="150" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-57" value="problems" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#f8cecc;strokeColor=#b85450;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-225" y="130" width="130" height="40" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-58" value="solutions" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-225" y="178" width="130" height="40" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-59" value="<b><font style="font-size: 24px;">Legend</font></b>" style="text;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-213" y="88" width="60" height="30" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-60" value="Do we need to allow the org to unsubscribe? We could do that, as it revokes the rights of a user or organisation to an application." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="90" y="1138" width="250" height="100" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-61" value="The subscription should contain a list of default roles which a user is given when they first sign into an application." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="-260" y="1180" width="250" height="80" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-62" value="If a user wants to create their own application and use abstrauth as the authentication server, then they mark the client as being private to their organisation, and so no other organisation can use it. A subscription is still created from the org to the app." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="420" y="1098" width="250" height="120" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-63" value="If a user wants their application to be used by other organisations, they mark it as public, so that subscriptions can be created." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects height="80" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aVcNS0bVbbwKASJEhnXi-64" value="Users who are invited to organisations get access to applications by having the roles added when they are added to the organisation.&nbsp;<div>When a user is created, the creating user creates clients&amp;roles for them.</div><div>Admins can manage clients&amp;roles for all applications that the org subscribes to</div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects div.div:
During initial sign-in in an abstratium app, the user is redirected to abstrauth. Abstrauth knows the clientId and hence the application. After creating the org, it can check if the client allows automatic subscription and if it does, it can create that subscription.
The JWT contains the orgId and that can be used as the tenantId in Hibernate.

switch.text: During initial sign-in in an abstratium a...

div.div: Paid features can be based on the user having specific roles. The roles are assigned during sign-in, by using the subscription to check the contract and its billing status. If the user has credit, has prepaid or we trust them to post-pay, then the extra roles are added to the JWT and the application can trust that they may use those features.
